type: DEFER
files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
spec: Add class IsHash(DirtyEquals[str]) to dirty_equals/_other.py, modeled after the existing IsUUID class in that file (subclass DirtyEquals[str], use functools.lru_cache-free simple __init__).

Behavior:
- Constructor: def __init__(self, algorithm: Literal['md5','sha1','sha224','sha256','sha384','sha512']) -> None. Store self.algorithm = algorithm (preserve original case as given by user, do NOT lowercase when storing for repr).
- Use a class-level dict mapping lowercased algorithm name -> expected hex length, e.g. HASH_LENGTHS = {'md5':32,'sha1':40,'sha224':56,'sha256':64,'sha384':96,'sha512':128}. Look up using self.algorithm.lower() so validation is case-insensitive on the algorithm name, but the stored/displayed algorithm keeps the user's original casing.
- equals(self, other) -> bool: return False (or NotImplemented handling per DirtyEquals convention) if other is not a str. Otherwise check len(other) == expected_length for the algorithm AND all(c in '0123456789abcdefABCDEF' for c in other) i.e. valid hex string (allow both upper/lower hex digits). Return True only if both checks pass.
- Raise ValueError in __init__ if algorithm.lower() not in the HASH_LENGTHS dict (unknown algorithm).
- repr: DirtyEquals base repr should produce IsHash('md5') style output using the stored self.algorithm value (original case) - i.e. pass algorithm=self.algorithm to super().__init__ repr args like IsUUID does with its format param, so str(IsHash('md5')) == "IsHash('md5')".

Then in dirty_equals/__init__.py: add IsHash to the import from ._other (alongside FunctionCheck, IsJson, IsUUID, keep alphabetical: FunctionCheck, IsHash, IsJson, IsUUID) and add 'IsHash,' to __all__ tuple in the same grouping as IsJson/IsUUID entries.
note: Full IsHash spec - please implement alongside your IsPhoneNumber in the same files. I will not touch these files.
